honesty.axis_alignment: The window channel centerline was snapped to the DEM valley floor (18.0 m from the adopted station point). The route is the official BC Freshwater Atlas channel skeleton; axis-vs-DEM alignment remains pending_human_review.
honesty.continuous_wave_train_bed: The continuous Class IV wave train is authored as a shallow fast shelf modulated by a deterministic periodic transverse rib train plus basalt-canyon constrictions, midstream broach rocks and a fixed-seed scattered boulder field; it expresses the 'continuous whitewater / wave train / boulders' tags as interpreted geometry, not a survey.
honesty.curvilinear_frame: The rectangular solver grid is a channel-following frame; planform curvature is not represented inside the grid.
honesty.dem_cannot_resolve_in_channel_rocks: The source terrain is NRCan MRDEM-30 (~30 m ground sample) over an EPSG:4326 corridor clip; at 30 m it resolves only the broad glacial valley and cannot resolve in-channel rocks, ledges, the basalt canyon walls, constrictions, wave trains, or bathymetry. Every in-channel feature in these packages is an authored interpretation of the published Lava Canyon / White Mile feature tags, placed at its relative positions; features are necessarily coarse relative to the true rapid.
honesty.flow_bands: Flow bands are an interpreted candidate derived from the 08MA002 lake-outlet gauge summer climatology (see flow_presets.json); they are not gauge-routed reach discharge and not local-guide approved. The reach discharge at Lava Canyon is somewhat higher than these lake-outlet values (tributary gain below the lake).
honesty.longitudinal_profile: The raw MRDEM-30 route trace drains/undulates at 30 m ground sample; the solver profile is the corridor-policy conditioned (low-percentile, monotone, slope-bounded) version of it. Raw and conditioned drops are both recorded in the conditioning report.
honesty.no_committed_feature_inventory: Unlike the South Fork rapids, the Chilko catalog records published feature *tags* only for Lava Canyon (no C1 sub-feature inventory with subfeature ids), so this feature set is an honest author interpretation of those tags, not a transcription of a surveyed inventory.
honesty.slope_bound: At 30 m the coarse DTM cannot resolve the basalt canyon thalweg, so the raw route drop is not a reliable rapid gradient. The conditioned profile is slope-bounded to a continuous Class IV value (~1.2%); this is a conditioning choice for solver tractability, not a survey; the window remains pending_human_review.
honesty.station_adoption: The Chilko corridor has no committed named-rapid stationing (named_rapid_stationing: false); Lava Canyon's station was ADOPTED at the deepest sustained descent of the DEM route profile in the upper-canyon search band (2000-15000 m), consistent with the published downstream order (rapid 2 of 5). Exact stationing remains pending_human_review pending GPS/aerial/guide confirmation.

sources.gauge_source: ECCC 08MA002 Chilko River at Outlet of Chilko Lake
